edge_cases.edge_case_description: The wording 'can directly support' and 'where implemented' makes 100 Kbps support appear optional or configuration-dependent, creating a verification edge case around whether normal-mode support is required in all delivered instances or may be absent in some implementations.

The phrase 'where correct hardware is included' makes 400 Kbps fast-mode support conditional on external implementation details, creating a verification edge case for builds or platforms where that hardware is absent and fast-mode behaviour is therefore undefined or not required.

The wording 'can support' does not state whether 400 Kbps support is mandatory for every implementation, creating an edge case over whether fast-mode must be verified universally or only in selected configurations.

The phrase 'can be set to either active high or active low' implies a configurable reset polarity, creating a verification edge case for both polarity settings because reset assertion/deassertion behaviour depends on the ARST_LVL configuration.

Because the requirement uses 'can be set' without strong mandatory language, it is unclear whether support for both active-high and active-low reset configurations is required in all implementations or whether only one polarity may be provided.
